description: Complete hostapd command and workflow reference from basic to advanced wireless access point configuration

commands.explanation: Displays the help message with all available command-line options and syntax
Prints the current version of hostapd to verify installation and build details
Starts the access point in the foreground using a specific configuration file
Starts hostapd in daemon mode (background) to run as a service
Runs in background and writes the process ID to a specific file for service management
Starts hostapd with verbose debugging enabled to show basic status and event messages
Enables excessive debugging to view detailed state machine transitions and frame details
Prepends timestamps to all debug output for timeline analysis of connection events
Redirects all log output to a specified file instead of standard output
Includes cryptographic key data in debug output (Warning: Unsafe for production, used for deep inspection)
Enables the global control interface for external management tools like hostapd_cli

commands.example: Generate minimal open config and run
commands.explanation: Creates and runs a basic Open (unencrypted) 2.4GHz access point on channel 6
Configures the AP to bridge traffic to a wired interface (br0) for network integration

commands.example: Generate hidden SSID config and run
commands.explanation: Creates an access point with a hidden SSID by disabling beacon SSID broadcasting
Sets up a standard WPA2 Personal network using CCMP (AES) encryption

commands.example: Generate WPA1/WPA2 Mixed config and run
commands.explanation: Enables mixed WPA1/WPA2 mode allowing both TKIP and CCMP ciphers (Legacy support)

commands.command:
printf 'interface=wlan0
ssid=MacFilterAP
macaddr_acl=1
accept_mac_file=hostapd.accept
' > hostapd.conf && echo '00:11:22:33:44:55' > hostapd.accept && hostapd hostapd.conf
commands.example: Generate MAC Allow List config and run
commands.explanation: Configures an Allow List (Whitelist) where only specified MAC addresses can associate

commands.command:
printf 'interface=wlan0
ssid=DenyListAP
macaddr_acl=0
deny_mac_file=hostapd.deny
' > hostapd.conf && echo 'aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff' > hostapd.deny && hostapd hostapd.conf
commands.example: Generate MAC Deny List config and run
commands.explanation: Configures a Deny List (Blacklist) to explicitly block specific devices from connecting
Enables 802.11n High Throughput mode with 40MHz channel width
Enables 802.11ac Very High Throughput mode on 5GHz with 80MHz channel width
Enables 802.11ax (Wi-Fi 6) High Efficiency features including beamforming support

commands.example: Generate Country Code config and run
commands.explanation: Advertises the US country code (802.11d) to clients to enforce regulatory domain constraints

commands.example: Generate WPA3-SAE Only config and run
commands.explanation: Configures a pure WPA3 network using SAE authentication and mandatory Management Frame Protection (MFP)

commands.example: Generate WPA3/WPA2 Transition config and run
commands.explanation: Allows both WPA3-SAE and WPA2-PSK clients to connect with optional MFP
Sets up Opportunistic Wireless Encryption (Enhanced Open) for encrypted unauthenticated connections
Configures WPA2-Enterprise requiring an external RADIUS server for 802.1X authentication

commands.example: Generate Internal EAP Server config and run
commands.explanation: Uses hostapd's built-in EAP server to handle 802.1X authentication without an external RADIUS

commands.example: Generate RADIUS Accounting config and run
commands.explanation: Enables RADIUS accounting to report session duration and data usage to an accounting server

commands.example: Generate AP Isolation config and run
commands.explanation: Prevents associated wireless clients from communicating directly with each other (Client Isolation)

commands.command:
printf 'interface=wlan0
ssid=WPS-Enabled
wps_state=2
eap_server=1
config_methods=label display push_button keypad
' > hostapd.conf && hostapd hostapd.conf
commands.explanation: Enables Wi-Fi Protected Setup (WPS) with Push Button and PIN methods (insecure but useful for testing)
Creates multiple virtual access points (SSIDs) on a single physical wireless interface

commands.example: Generate Custom Beacon Interval config and run
commands.explanation: Reduces the beacon interval to 15 TU (approx 15ms) for aggressive network advertising
Enables Karma mode (if using a patched hostapd-mana) to respond to all probe requests

commands.example: Generate Rogue AP config and run
commands.explanation: Sets up a Rogue AP mimicking a corporate network and logging full details including keys for interception

commands.example: Generate Strict MFP config and run
commands.explanation: Enforces Management Frame Protection (PMF) to block deauthentication attacks

commands.example: Generate Dynamic VLAN config and run
commands.explanation: Configures dynamic VLAN assignment based on RADIUS attributes or station MAC addresses
Pings the running hostapd daemon to verify the control interface is responsive
Lists all currently associated stations (clients) along with their statistics
Forces a specific client to disconnect from the access point immediately
Disassociates a client, effectively kicking them off but allowing immediate reconnection attempts
Dynamically updates the path to the MAC deny list file without restarting the AP
Reloads the configuration file to apply changes like updated MAC lists
Activates WPS Push Button Configuration mode for the next two minutes
Registers a specific WPS PIN to allow a client to connect using that PIN
Retrieves and displays the current runtime configuration details of the AP
Dynamically changes the logging verbosity to DEBUG while the AP is running
Runs hostapd logging keys and timestamps to a file for offline packet decryption analysis
Shows detailed statistics for a single connected client including signal strength and packet counts

commands.example: Generate Basic Passpoint config and run
commands.explanation: Enables Interworking bits to advertise Hotspot 2.0 capabilities to compatible clients
Initiates a Channel Switch Announcement (CSA) moving the AP to a new frequency after 5 beacons

commands.example: Generate WMM AIFS config and run
commands.explanation: Tunes WMM parameters (AIFS) to prioritize Voice/Video traffic or enforce airtime fairness
Deploys a honeypot AP that logs all events to syslog for SIEM ingestion and incident simulation

commands.example: Generate DFS Channel config and run
commands.explanation: Configures the AP on a DFS channel (52) requiring radar detection (802.11h) enabled
Enables Fast Transition (FT) to allow clients to roam quickly between APs in the same mobility domain
Adjusts RTS and Fragmentation thresholds to manage collisions in noisy environments

commands.example: Generate Max Clients config and run
commands.explanation: Limits the maximum number of associated stations to 10 to force load balancing to other APs

commands.command: hostapd -B hostapd.conf && sleep 5 && hostapd_cli ping && echo 'AP Started'
commands.explanation: Automation sequence to start hostapd, wait for initialization, and verify responsiveness
Stops all running hostapd instances immediately
Cleans up stale control interface sockets to prevent binding errors on restart

commands.command: ip link set wlan0 down && ip addr flush dev wlan0
commands.explanation: Resets the wireless interface state to default after stopping the access point
